abort.already_configured: Lock Code Manager config entry with same slugified name already exists.
abort.locks_updated: The locks for your config entry have been updated.

error.excluded_platform: Entities from the `{integration}` integration are not supported as condition entities. See the [wiki]({docs_url}) for details.
error.invalid_config: Slots configuration is invalid. For more information, check the logs and the docs.
error.missing_pin_if_enabled: PIN must be set if slot is enabled.
error.slots_already_configured: The following slots for lock {lock} are already managed by another config entry ({entry_title}) and can't be added to this configuration without being removed from the other one: {common_slots}

choose_path.description:
Some locks mask stored PINs, making them invisible to external tools. Once LCM is managing your locks, do not set codes using other tools or manually at the lock. Clear all slots before setup. For permanent access codes, configure them through LCM without a condition entity.

Choose whether you want to configure each slot via the UI or if you want to configure all slots in a YAML config.
choose_path.title: Choose Path

code_slot.description:
**Configure slot {slot_num}:**{existing_code_msg}

A slot is active when all conditions are met: the condition entity (if configured) is 'on', and Number of Uses (if configured) is greater than 0. For calendars, 'on' means an event is in progress. Supported entity types: calendar, binary_sensor, switch, schedule, input_boolean.
code_slot.title: Configure Slot {slot_num}

reauth.description: The lock `{lock}`, in the configuration for `{name}`, is no longer configured in Home Assistant and must be removed from your configuration. Choose new locks that this configuration is for. If you no longer need this configuration, delete the configuration entry.
reauth.title: Update locks for {name}

ui.description: Configure slots for all codes you want on the lock (existing user codes for the slot range specified will be cleared). You can add and remove slots later using the integration services.
ui.title: How Many Slots?

user.description: Give this configuration a unique name (e.g. `House Locks`) and select the locks you would like to manage with it.
user.title: Lock Code Manager

yaml.description: The config is expected to be a dictionary where the key is the slot number. For more information, check the docs.
yaml.title: Configure slots

error.invalid_config: Slots configuration is invalid. For more information, check the logs and the docs.
error.locks_already_configured: The following lock(s) are already managed by another config entry: {locks}.

init.description: Update the locks that are associated with this config entry and/or the slot configurations.
init.title: Lock Code Manager

hard_refresh_usercodes.description: If Lock Code Manager supports the lock's integration, and the lock's integration supports it, this will query the lock for all usercodes. This is potentially useful for integrations that cache data from the lock and a usercode(s) is configured outside of Home Assistant.
hard_refresh_usercodes.name: Hard refresh usercodes

init.title: Migrate from Number of Uses
init.description:
The built-in **Number of Uses** feature is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Use the **[Slot Usage Limiter blueprint]({blueprint_url})** instead. It provides the same functionality with added features like notifications and unlimited mode.

**Warning:** Clicking **Submit** will permanently remove Number of Uses from your configuration. Only do this after you have set up the blueprint replacement.

init.title: Slot {slot_num} was disabled

pin_required.title: Lock Code Manager: PIN required for slot {slot_num}

init.title: PIN required for slot {slot_num}
init.description:
A PIN is required to enable slot {slot_num} on the lock configuration **{config_entry_title}**. Please set a PIN before enabling this slot.

Click **Submit** to acknowledge this issue.

lock_offline.title: Lock unreachable: {lock_entity_id}
lock_offline.description: Lock Code Manager has been unable to communicate with `{lock_entity_id}` for multiple consecutive attempts. Code synchronization is paused until the lock is reachable again. This issue will be automatically dismissed when the lock recovers.
